Are you my student?

My work ethos

I love to make my own tools

I believe that making your own open source design tool is the first step to build a resilient practice without Adobe.

p5.zine is a tool for making an interactive and generative zine with p5.js
p5.zine — openprocessing.org ↗
A tool for creating books with code. It can export to PDF with saddle stitch and bleed settings.
p5.book — editor.p5js.org ↗

I was able to produce the 50 Books x 50 Covers Exhibition this semester using my own custom tool. Another alternative is possible.

practice

My Practice

I work with many mission-aligned organizations and independent researchers to build design systems and digital work.

  • AIGA
    AIGA — imageofthestudio.athleticsnyc.io ↗
  • New Museum
    New Museum — newmuseum.org ↗
  • Processing Foundation
    Processing Foundation — processingfoundation.org ↗
  • Robert Blackburn Printmaking Workshop
    Robert Blackburn — blackburnoralhistoryproject.org ↗
  • Just Tech Fellow
    Just Tech — todd-dev.free99.work ↗
  • Gallery of Contemporary Art
    Gallery of Contemporary Art — www.goca.gallery ↗
  • National Taiwan University
  • New York Review of Books
  • Secret Riso Club
  • Asian Labor Future Institute
  • Black Zine Fair
